summary:
I found and fixed the pagination bug in the Flask application.

## The Problem
The application had an off-by-one error in its pagination logic. The offset calculation in `app/app.py` was incorrectly subtracting 1 for pages after the first page:
```python
offset = (page - 1) * per_page - (1 if page > 1 else 0)
```

This caused page 2 to start at ID 10 instead of ID 11, resulting in item overlap between pages.

## The Fix
I corrected the offset calculation to use the standard pagination formula:
```python
offset = (page - 1) * per_page
```

## Verification
Running the test script confirms the fix works correctly:
```
PASS: Pagination is correct across page boundaries
```

The test verifies that:
- Page 1 ends at ID 10
- Page 2 starts at ID 11 (no gaps, no overlap)
- Both pages contain exactly 10 items each

The pagination now works correctly across all page boundaries.
